title: The Alliance Says Farewell to Wano
description: On pages 9–11, this part focuses on Wano Citizens, Unknown person #7, Unknown person #8, Unknown person #9, Orochi, Kaido as Wano celebrates liberation while Momonosuke becomes shogun, Green Bull threatens the island, and the alliance prepares to depart.
scene: Wano celebrates liberation while Momonosuke becomes shogun, Green Bull threatens the island, and the alliance prepares to depart.

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: Is that Wano...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: ...under- water?!

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: That is an older wano...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: From about 800 years ago.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Exactly as I said. Although as to why it ended up up up like this...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: ...I have no idea.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But it's so well preserved, does that mean this isn't really sea water?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: This is what I do know;

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Long ago, Wano was much larger and located at sea level...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...on the land around the base of Mount Fiji.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: At some point, walls were erected all around, enclosing the island.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As a result, the rainwater had no place to escape to,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and whole towns were eventually submerged and abandoned.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "The land further up mountain was established for settlers,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And that grew into the country we are familiar with today.

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: The current Wano!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: precisely.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: We have arrived.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: think

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: This is the Lode PoneGlyph.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: It's really here....!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: This is our third.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We just need one more to travel to laugh tale....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We are in a cavern near the foot of Mount Fiji.

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: Old Wano

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you continued to trek a little deeper...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Current location

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...it is said that you would find the sleeping ancient weapon, PLUTION.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I have never seen it myself and am unable to show it to you.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Retriieving Pluton would require...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The removal of the walls that surround this nation.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: 'Opening the country' is quite a literal act. We would lose our greatest defense...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Opening the country unleashes Pluton?!

dialogue.speaker: Orochi
dialogue.text: Why would Kozukii Open Want that?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Everything I have told you was passed down through generations of shopunate rule...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I can not speak to what open may have learned himself...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: during his voyages in the outside world.

dialogue.speaker: Wano Citizens
dialogue.text: The flower capital

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: No man can hope to stand against the forces of nature alone!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Get it? If Kaido was still in-charge,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Wouldn't have come here!!

summary: This part advances the Wano Aftermath through 42 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
